title: Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken

seo.title: How to Make Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken — Easy Korean Recipe
seo.metaDescription: Learn how to make authentic Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken. This easy Korean recipe includes step-by-step instructions, tips, and serving ideas.
longTailKeywords: how to make Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken
easy Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken recipe
authentic Korean Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken
lowCompetitionKeywords: Korean Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken recipe
seo.h1: Authentic Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken Recipe
h2Headings: What is Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken?

seo.imageAlt: Authentic Korean Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken recipe
seo.imageCaption: Delicious homemade Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken — a beloved Korean classic

description: Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken is a beloved dish featuring perfectly crisp chicken coated in a luscious, sweet, spicy, and tangy sauce. Each bite offers an irresistible crunch followed by a burst of complex flavors that are truly addictive. This iconic Korean comfort food is perfect for sharing with friends and family.

instructions.title: Prepare the Chicken
instructions.text: Pat the chicken pieces very dry with paper towels. Season them with salt, black pepper, and ginger powder. In a large bowl, whisk the egg.

instructions.title: Make the Batter
instructions.text: To the bowl with the whisked egg, add the potato starch, all-purpose flour, and cold water. Mix until a smooth, thick batter forms. Add the seasoned chicken pieces to the batter, ensuring each piece is fully coated.

instructions.title: Prepare the Yangnyeom Sauce
instructions.text: In a saucepan, combine the gochujang, soy sauce, rice vinegar, sugar, minced garlic, minced ginger, sesame oil, and 2 tablespoons of water. Bring the mixture to a gentle simmer over medium-low heat, stirring constantly until the sugar dissolves and the sauce thickens slightly, about 3-5 minutes. Set aside.

instructions.title: First Fry the Chicken
instructions.text: Heat the frying oil in a deep pot or Dutch oven to 170°C (340°F). Carefully add the battered chicken pieces, frying in batches to avoid overcrowding. Fry for 6-7 minutes until light golden brown and cooked through. Remove the chicken with a slotted spoon and place on a wire rack to drain.

instructions.title: Second Fry for Crispy Texture
instructions.text: Increase the oil temperature to 180°C (350°F). Return the partially fried chicken to the hot oil for a second fry, again in batches. Fry for another 3-4 minutes until deeply golden brown and extra crispy. Remove and drain on a wire rack.

instructions.title: Coat with Yangnyeom Sauce
instructions.text: In a large bowl, add the freshly fried, crispy chicken. Pour the prepared Yangnyeom sauce over the chicken and gently toss until all pieces are evenly coated. Work quickly to ensure the chicken remains crispy.

instructions.title: Garnish and Serve
instructions.text: Transfer the coated chicken to a serving platter. Garnish generously with sesame seeds and chopped green onions. Serve immediately and enjoy the perfect balance of sweet, spicy, and savory flavors.

tips: For truly crispy chicken, do not skip the double frying method. The first fry cooks the chicken through, and the second fry at a higher temperature creates that signature crunch.
Adjust the amount of gochujang to control the spice level according to your preference. For a thicker, shinier sauce, you can use corn syrup or honey instead of granulated sugar.

Yangnyeom chicken is best enjoyed immediately while hot and crispy. If you have leftovers, reheat them quickly in an air fryer or oven to regain some crispiness, though it won't be quite the same as fresh.
